user_message: Can you work with me on territory planning for Litware Inc?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 17:31 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 13:15 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-N trap (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-N failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-N -- Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
